overview: This step joins all segments of each source and/or target text unit into a single content, effectively reversing prior segmentation. Non-segmented entries and text units flagged as non-translatable are left untouched. It takes filter events and sends filter events.

desegmentSource.help: Joins all segments within each text unit's source content back into a single unsegmented content. Non-segmented entries and non-translatable text units are left untouched.

desegmentTarget.help: Joins all segments within each text unit's target content back into a single unsegmented content. Only applies when a target text exists; entries without a target are left untouched.

renumberCodes.help: Restores inline code IDs to their original values after desegmentation. Use this when the content was previously segmented with the **Renumber code IDs** option in the Segmentation step, which renumbers code IDs to a sequential `1, 2, 3…` within each segment. This option reverses that renumbering so the merged content has the original ID sequence.
notes: Only meaningful when the content was previously segmented using the **Renumber code IDs** option in the Segmentation step. If codes were not renumbered during segmentation, this option has no effect.

processingNotes: Non-segmented entries pass through untouched — no error is raised.
Text units flagged as **non-translatable** are skipped entirely.
Both source and target languages are required in the pipeline context.

examples.title: Reverse segmentation in a round-trip pipeline
examples.description: Typically used after translation to rejoin segments that were split by the Segmentation step earlier in the pipeline. When the Segmentation step used **Renumber code IDs**, enable **Restore original IDs to renumbered codes** in this step to recover the original code ID sequence.
examples.config:
```yaml
desegmentSource: true
desegmentTarget: true
renumberCodes: true
```
